calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Set For Trial On Aug-31-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 26, 2025, in Department 304, Line 2.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Joy Global Underground Mining LLC's Motion to Quash Plaintiffs' Complaint is OFF CALENDAR pursuant to Notice of Withdrawal filed August 22, 2025. = (EPS/304).

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Continued To Aug-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 304
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Counsel Andrew Chew for Plaintiff; and Counsel Todd Thacker for Defendant Joy Global Underground Mining LLC.

At 9:00AM, Case is called, on the record counsel states their appearances. This morning's proceedings are reported. The court and counsel discuss stipulation and request for continuance.

The Court notes that no stipulation and request to continue the hearing was received or filed with the Court prior to the scheduled appearance.

Counsel are admonished that any request for a continuance must be submitted in accordance with applicable court rules and procedures, and filed timely with the Court. The failure to do so results in unnecessary expenditure of judicial resources and may cause inconvenience to the parties and the Court.

The Court expects compliance with procedural requirements in all future matters.

Counsel provides the court with status of the case and request continuance.

Upon conclusion, the court modifies its tentative ruling and rules as follows:

The Court GRANTS request for continuance, DEFENDANT JOY GLOBAL UNDERGROUND MINING LLC's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is CONTINUED to August 26, 2025, at 9:00AM, in Department 304. Counsel to submit stipulation and order for the court's review and signature. (304/EPS).
